title: TON jumps 15% as The Open Network plans rebrand to Gram
summary: Telegram founder Pavel Durov says it is “returning to our roots — and starting a new chapter” by rebranding Toncoin to Gram.

title: Bitmine buys $52M ETH as Tom Lee says price not yet showing Ethereum’s strength
summary: Bitmine is aiming to hold 5% of the total circulating supply of 120.6 million Ether tokens and is about 90% of the way to its target after its latest purchase.

title: Stocks Pare Losses to Hold Near Record, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks recovered much of their decline as investors bought the dip after a pullback in the AI-driven rally that has been one of the market’s dominant themes this year.

title: Steph Curry Signs Deal With Chinese Sportswear Giant
summary: Basketball star Stephen Curry signed a 10-year endorsement deal with Chinese sportswear giant Li Ning. Its success depends on how Li Ning positions stores, products and marketing, says Bloomberg Intelligence's Catherine Lim. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nvidia Releases Its Best Open AI Model Yet—But Still Lags Behind China
summary: Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Ultra tops every American open-weight AI system by a wide margin—but still trails the Chinese-led frontier.

title: DuckDuckGo Launched Duck AI. Now Their Hit Product is 'No AI'
summary: As Google buries its search results under AI-generated answers, DuckDuckGo believes a growing number of users just want the old internet back.

title: Bitcoin slides toward $70,000 as Strategy’s BTC sale, geopolitical risks weigh on crypto
summary: Analysts said the size of Strategy's bitcoin sale was relatively trivial, but sent a bearish signal to the broader market.

title: Robinhood closes $180 million WonderFi acquisition, entering Canadian crypto market
summary: Users of WonderFi's crypto platforms Bitbuy and Coinsquare will be invited to continue on the Robinhood app.

title: How did Spain’s unemployment rate converge with Finland’s?
